raw.title: Meet the artist whose decoys are rebuilding the world’s seabird colonies
raw.excerpt: For more than a decade, Sue “Seabird Sue” Schubel’s lifelike decoys have anchored a global seabird restoration effort built on “social attraction,” luring colony-nesting birds back to lost or safer habitats, a strategy that has aided about a third of the world’s seabird species, including some of the most endangered.

llm_processed.summary: Artist Sue Schubel's decoy birds are helping to rebuild the world's seabird colonies, demonstrating the power of social attraction in conservation efforts. This approach recognizes the importance of community-based solutions and collaborative conservation.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: Using decoy birds as a conservation tool could enable unexpected bridges with community-led conservation projects, as well as research on more effective and inclusive conservation strategies.
